source.description: Six-panel raster marker contact sheet generated for an honest backdated June 16, 2026 envelope sticker lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet must keep one envelope sticker design and visible marker texture across the sequence.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the envelope, flap, face, stamp, shadow, and color areas before the final panel only strengthens existing marker work.

final_elements.name: rounded envelope body
final_elements.description: A wide rounded envelope sticker body.

final_elements.name: folded flap lines
final_elements.description: Diagonal flap lines and a central flap point on the envelope.

final_elements.name: thick black outline
final_elements.description: A bold marker outline around the envelope body.

final_elements.name: cute face
final_elements.description: Two small eyes and a friendly smile on the envelope.

final_elements.name: tiny stamp heart
final_elements.description: A small coral heart stamp in the upper corner.

final_elements.name: sticker shadow
final_elements.description: A simple offset shadow that makes the envelope feel like a sticker.

final_elements.name: yellow and coral marker fills
final_elements.description: Warm yellow envelope fill with visible marker strokes.

final_elements.name: tiny shine marks
final_elements.description: Small highlights on existing envelope and stamp shapes.

frames.visible_job: Draws the wide rounded rectangle that becomes the envelope sticker body.
Adds diagonal flap lines and thickens the existing envelope outline.
Adds the cute face and tiny heart stamp.
Adds a simple offset sticker shadow beneath the existing envelope.
Colors existing envelope and stamp shapes and adds tiny shine marks.

final_step.visible_job: Retraces the existing outline, evens marker fills, and clarifies the face, stamp, shadow, and highlights already introduced.

rejection_checks: No envelope flap, face, stamp, shadow, color area, or highlight should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink or color a shape that was not already visible earlier.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same envelope pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
